patches.find: Only {{CA_LIVE}} WINs fetch live external data, and even those use adaptive tolerances with 50% error floors.
patches.replace: Only {{CA_LIVE}} WINs fetch live external data — all from globe-calibrated sources — and the NMP Drift Rate domain uses an adaptive tolerance with a 50% error floor.
patches.rationale: EXP-103 PATCH-1-TLDR: The 50% error floor applies only to the NMP Drift Rate domain (WIN-006/007). WIN-004 and WIN-024 have different tolerance settings. Overgeneralizing to all 4 live-fetch WINs is factually wrong and exploitable by a dome defender who inspects WIN-004 settings.

patches.find: Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions with no measurable content (WIN-032 New Jerusalem, WIN-034 copper firmament) — monitoring is not applicable. Nine are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-045/046/049/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. {{TALLY_MISLEADING}} are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). The remaining WINs are quantitative predictions the dome could monitor but chooses not to. The absence is most telling for the self-contradicted WINs: the dome has built monitoring infrastructure for {{CA_HARDCODED}} WINs (all hardcoded to pass) but does not monitor the 9 WINs whose own geometry produces wrong answers.
patches.replace:
Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions with no measurable content (WIN-032, WIN-034) — monitoring is not applicable. Eight are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity, WIN-068 tidal model) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. Fifteen are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). Eleven are standard-physics relabellings — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome's causal explanation. Three are refuted by published data — monitoring would expose the failure. Two are not demonstrated. The gaps are systematic: the dome's monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.

The contrast with the hardcoded WINs is even more revealing. Of the dome's 16 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), eight have no monitoring at all — but the other eight (WIN-002/010/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass. The dome does not merely neglect to test its geometric contradictions; for half of them, it builds validation infrastructure that cannot fail by construction. Whether omitted or hardcoded, no self-contradicted WIN faces a genuine empirical test.
patches.rationale: EXP-103 PATCH-2: Fix self-contradicted count (9→8, missing WIN-044/068, wrongly included WIN-045/046/049 which are hardcoded not none), fix misleading count (template var→15 verified from wins.json), add full verdict breakdown for unmonitored WINs (11 SME, 3 RBD, 2 ND), replace contrast sentence with stronger framing (dome actively hardcodes SC WINs rather than just neglecting them). All counts verified against current wins.json.
